canonical_rules.canonical_rule: 44 agents are the baseline. Upgrade/remap existing agents first. Create new agents only when a real role gap remains after mapping.
canonical_rules.documentation_rule: All documentation is Markdown. Flow diagrams are Mermaid. Machine-readable source of truth comes first.
canonical_rules.publication_gate_rule: Do not redesign or touch publication gates in this mapping. This maps upstream agent, editorial, visual, learning, and channel responsibilities.

allowed_only_if: No existing agent has a matching owner_surface.
No existing agent can be upgraded without role confusion.
The function requires independent state, receipts, cadence, and failure modes.

verified_external_patterns.callscore_use: callscore-cmo-head supervises editorial/channel allocation; callscore-orchestrator-head supervises runtime control.
Route by channel and asset type.
Channel heads delegate to posting, visual, analytics, data, evidence, and review workers.
Review, trust, compliance, visual QA, and platform-fit agents reject or return work for revision before gate handoff.
Restricted actions and editorial uncertainty can pause without weakening existing gates.
Persistent planning, memory, skills, filesystem artifacts, and subagent delegation.
